nativeRange.habitat: Shallow, heavily vegetated areas of Lake Sentani; a highland lake at approximately 75 m elevation in the Jayapura region of West Papua / Irian Jaya

decorPreferences: Dense planting at sides and back for cover and egg-laying sites
Open central swimming area for active schooling
Spawning mop or fine-leaved plants (Java moss, Cabomba) for breeding
Moderate lighting with some shaded areas

diet.notes: Generally accepts a wide range of foods including quality flakes, small pellets, and live or frozen fare such as bloodworms, brine shrimp, and daphnia. Including vegetable-based foods alongside protein-rich items tends to support health and color development. Has a relatively small mouth for its body size — smaller-cut foods are recommended.

avoidWith: Dwarf shrimp (may be eaten or harassed)

summary: The Red Rainbowfish (Glossolepis incisus) is a striking freshwater schooling fish from Lake Sentani in northern New Guinea. Males develop an intense, solid red body coloration as they mature — a process that takes one to two years. Groups of six or more, along with hard water, bring out the best color.
careNotes: Males of this species are widely considered among the most vividly colored freshwater fish in the hobby, developing a solid, intense red body coloration as they mature. Full adult color generally takes 1–2 years to develop; juveniles and young adults tend to appear silvery with only a hint of red. Females remain silvery-pink throughout their lives and are noticeably less colorful. For best color expression, a group of 6 or more individuals is generally advised — males display more intensely when kept with other males and females. Hard, moderately alkaline water consistent with the species' native Lake Sentani environment tends to support health and breeding condition. An active, fast swimmer that benefits from a tank at least 120 cm in length with open central swimming space and planted margins. Despite its size and activity level, this species is peaceful toward similarly sized or larger tankmates. May consume dwarf shrimp or very small fry. The species is listed as Endangered (EN) on the IUCN Red List; captive-bred stock is widely available and preferred. A tightly-fitted lid is recommended, as the species has been reported to jump. Cross-reference: Boesemani Rainbow (fw-091) and Neon Dwarf Rainbow share similar care requirements and make compatible tankmates in large community aquariums. Note: also commonly sold simply as 'Red Rainbow' — distinct from the Red Neon Rainbowfish (fw-fish-216, Pseudomugil luminatus), a tiny pseudomugilid blue-eye, not a Melanotaeniid.
breedingNotes: Egg-scatterer; deposits adhesive eggs among fine-leaved plants or spawning mops over extended spawning sessions. Eggs generally hatch in 7–8 days at typical aquarium temperatures. Fry are small and benefit from infusoria or commercial micro-fry foods initially, progressing to baby brine shrimp nauplii. Breeding itself is generally considered approachable for intermediate keepers; raising fry to adulthood tends to require more attention due to fry sensitivity and small initial size. A dedicated breeding tank with slightly raised temperature and alkaline water around pH 7.5 tends to improve spawning success.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish
primary.notes: Primary source; Aquarium Co-Op had no dedicated species page for this species (returned 404). SeriouslyFish used as primary. Provided tank size (110L / 120cm), temperature (22–25°C), pH (7.0–8.0), hardness (10–20°H), adult size (15 cm max SL), diet, breeding, native range, and compatibility.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, taxonomy (Melanotaeniidae / Atheriniformes), native range (Lake Sentani, Irian Jaya), adult size (males 12 cm SL), temperature (29–30°C wild optimal — notably higher than hobby sources; averaged into upper bound), pH (7.6–8.2), hardness (9–19 dH). IUCN Endangered status confirmed. Temperature max set to 28°C as a reasonable average between FishBase wild data (29–30°C) and hobby sources (25–26°C).

additional.name: Fishkeeping World
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (up to 15 cm), temperature (21–26°C), pH (6.5–8.5), gH (8–25 dGH), lifespan (6–8 years), tank size (30–60 gal = 114–227L), schooling minimum (5+). Escape risk (jumping) confirmed. Parameters consistent with SeriouslyFish on all major fields.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: No dedicated species page available; referenced general rainbowfish blog for supplementary context. Confirmed 4-foot minimum tank for school of 6+, adult size ~12 cm, hard alkaline water origin, small mouth size requiring appropriately sized foods.

fish.breedingNotes: The Red Rainbowfish scatters adhesive eggs among fine-leaved plants or spawning mops. Spawning is relatively straightforward, though raising fry to adulthood tends to be more demanding due to the small size of newly hatched fish.
